codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking TTL carry-over bug: `PolicyPromptModal` now resets countdown state when the active queued prompt id changes and performs auto-dismiss from the same effect, preventing stale `remainingMs=0` from immediately dismissing the next FIFO prompt. Added fake-timer tests cover head-expiry queue advance and mid-countdown queue advance reset behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is an execution gap between required agent-browser verification for modal changes and the ability to drive the demo queue state for this specific flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `ui-screen-worker` requires manual agent-browser verification for changed modals (`.factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md`, Work Procedure steps 6-7), but `MockAppStateProvider` exposes no `window.__appState`/debug handle (`src/app/MockAppStateProvider.tsx` has no `__appState` references) while only `AppStateProvider` publishes `window.__appState` (`src/app/AppStateProvider.tsx:2881-2930`). The worker therefore reported a justified deviation in handoff (`/Users/plebdev/.factory/missions/b48100dd-0e6c-4a7c-90a3-f12e61d1c3c4/handoffs/2026-04-21T20-57-41-991Z__fix-m2-policy-prompt-modal-ttl-reset__440c57a5-f0d7-414a-9cb3-fcd8196e290a.json`).

summary: PASS — This fix adequately resolves the original TTL-reset failure from `m2-reactive-policy-prompt-modal` by re-arming countdown state on FIFO prompt advancement and preventing premature auto-dismiss of newly active prompts.
